description: OpenLark - Open source workplace super-app (Lark/Feishu clone). SaaS-first, multi-tenant, real-time collaboration. Tech: Next.js 15, Fastify, PostgreSQL, Redis, Yjs+Hocuspocus, LiveKit, S3.

userStories.title: Scaffold monorepo with Turborepo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project scaffolded as a Turborepo monorepo so all packages share tooling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Turborepo monorepo with apps/web (Next.js 15 App Router), apps/api (Fastify), and packages/shared (TypeScript types/utils)
Configure TypeScript strict mode across all packages
Add ESLint + Prettier with shared configs
Add turbo.json with build, dev, lint, typecheck pipelines
pnpm install succeeds and turbo dev starts both apps
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scaffolded monorepo with apps/web (Next.js 15.5.12), apps/api (Fastify 5), packages/shared. TypeScript strict mode enabled. ESLint + Prettier configured. turbo.json pipelines: build, dev, lint, typecheck. pnpm install succeeds, turbo dev starts both apps (web:3000, api:3001), typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Database setup with Drizzle ORM
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PostgreSQL database connection and ORM configured.
acceptanceCriteria: Add drizzle-orm and drizzle-kit to apps/api
Create drizzle.config.ts pointing to DATABASE_URL env var
Create db/index.ts with connection pool setup
Add docker-compose.yml with postgres:16 and redis:7 services
docker compose up starts database successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added drizzle-orm 0.45.1, postgres 3.4.8, drizzle-kit 0.31.9. Created drizzle.config.ts with DATABASE_URL env var. Created src/db/index.ts with postgres-js connection pool (max 10 connections, 20s idle timeout). Added docker-compose.yml with postgres:16 and redis:7 services (openlark-postgres on 5432, openlark-redis on 6379). Added .env.example with DATABASE_URL template. Typecheck passes.

Create organizations table: id (uuid), name, domain (unique), logo_url, industry, plan (enum: starter/pro/enterprise), settings (jsonb), created_at, updated_at

userStories.notes: Created organizations table with uuid pk, name, domain (unique), logo_url, industry, plan enum (starter/pro/enterprise), settings jsonb, timestamps. Added users_org_id_idx index. Migration 0000_swift_absorbing_man.sql generated and applied successfully. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Department and membership schemas

userStories.notes: Created departments table with uuid pk, name, parent_id (self-referencing fk), org_id (fk), timestamps. Migration 0001_yellow_blink.sql generated and applied successfully. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Session and API key schemas

userStories.notes: Created sessions table with uuid pk, user_id fk (cascade delete), token_hash (unique, indexed), device_info jsonb, ip varchar(45), expires_at, created_at. Created api_keys table with uuid pk, org_id fk (cascade delete), user_id fk (cascade delete), key_hash (unique, indexed), scopes text[], name, last_used_at, created_at, revoked_at. Added indexes on token_hash, user_id for sessions; key_hash, org_id, user_id for api_keys. Migration 0002_lean_lenny_balinger.sql generated and applied successfully. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Auth API - Registration endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to register with email and password so I can create an account.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/register accepts {email, password, display_name}
Password hashed with bcrypt (12 rounds)
Returns 201 with user object (no password_hash) and session token
Returns 409 if email already exists
Validates email format and password min length (8 chars)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created apps/api/src/routes/auth.ts with POST /auth/register endpoint. Added bcrypt dependency for password hashing (12 rounds). Validates email format with regex and password min length (8 chars). Returns 409 for duplicate email. Auto-creates personal organization on registration. Creates session with 30-day expiry, returns user object (excluding password_hash) and session token. Token is 32 random bytes hex-encoded, stored as SHA-256 hash. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Auth API - Login endpoint
userStories.description: As a user, I want to login with email and password.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /auth/login accepts {email, password}
Returns 200 with user object and session token on success
Returns 401 with generic message on wrong email or password
Creates session record in database
Session token is a cryptographically random string (32 bytes hex)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented login endpoint with bcrypt password verification. Uses same session token generation (32 bytes random, SHA-256 hashed for storage) as registration.

userStories.title: Auth middleware and session validation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need middleware that validates session tokens on protected routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create auth middleware that reads Authorization: Bearer <token> header
Looks up session by token_hash, checks not expired
Attaches user and org to request context
Returns 401 if no token, invalid token, or expired session
POST /auth/logout invalidates the current session
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created apps/api/src/middleware/auth.ts with authMiddleware function. Reads Authorization: Bearer <token> header, hashes token with SHA-256 before lookup (prevents timing attacks), validates session exists and not expired, loads user (excluding passwordHash) and organization, attaches to request context via Fastify declaration merging. Added POST /auth/logout endpoint that deletes the current session. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Registration page UI

Client-side validation: email format, password match, min length
On submit, call POST /auth/register API
On success, redirect to /app (main dashboard)

userStories.notes: Created /register page with Tailwind CSS v4. Set up Tailwind via @tailwindcss/postcss plugin with globals.css importing tailwindcss. Form includes display name, email, password, and confirm password fields. Client-side validation checks email format (regex), password length (min 8), and password match. API calls go through Next.js rewrites to proxy to localhost:3001. Shows API errors in red alert box. Redirects to /app on success. Added /app placeholder page. Also created postcss.config.mjs and updated next.config.js with API rewrites.

userStories.title: Login page UI

On submit, call POST /auth/login API
Store session token in httpOnly cookie
On success, redirect to /app

userStories.notes: Created /login page following same patterns as registration page. Form includes email and password fields with client-side validation. On submit, calls POST /api/auth/login via Next.js API proxy. On success, stores session token in cookie and redirects to /app. On error, displays error message in red alert box. Includes link to /register for new users. Typecheck passes. Page verified rendering at localhost:3000/login with correct elements.

userStories.title: App shell layout with navigation rail

Navigation rail has icon buttons: Messenger, Calendar, Docs, Tasks, Base, Wiki, Email, Admin
Active nav item highlighted; clicking navigates to /app/[module]
User avatar + dropdown menu at bottom of nav rail (profile, settings, logout)
Redirect to /login if no valid session

userStories.notes: Created apps/web/src/app/app/layout.tsx with three-panel structure. Installed lucide-react and @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu. Navigation rail (56px, bg-gray-900) with 8 icon buttons: Messenger, Calendar, Docs, Tasks, Base, Wiki, Email, Admin using Lucide icons. Active nav item highlighted with blue background. User avatar at bottom with Radix dropdown menu containing Profile, Settings, and Logout options. Session validation via GET /auth/me endpoint (added to API); redirects to /login if no valid session. Resizable sidebar panel (120-400px range) with collapse/expand toggle. Typecheck passes for both web and api packages.

userStories.title: Organization creation and settings API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need APIs to create and manage organizations.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /orgs creates organization with {name, domain}; creator becomes primary admin
GET /orgs/:id returns organization details
PATCH /orgs/:id updates name, logo_url, industry, settings
Only primary admin or users with org.manage scope can update
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created apps/api/src/routes/orgs.ts with three endpoints. POST /orgs creates organization with name and optional domain, sets current user as primaryAdminId, and updates user's orgId. GET /orgs/:id returns organization details (only accessible to members). PATCH /orgs/:id updates name, logo_url, industry, settings - restricted to primary admin or users with org.manage scope via API key. Added primaryAdminId field to organizations schema. Migration 0003_dazzling_ezekiel_stane.sql applied. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: User invitation API
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to invite users to my organization.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /orgs/:id/invitations accepts {emails: string[]}
Creates invitation records with unique tokens
Returns invitation links
GET /auth/accept-invite/:token validates and shows accept page
POST /auth/accept-invite/:token creates user in org (or adds existing user to org)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created invitations table with id, org_id, email, token_hash, invited_by_id, status (pending/accepted/declined/revoked/expired), expires_at, accepted_at, created_at. Migration 0004_stale_ser_duncan.sql applied. POST /orgs/:id/invitations creates invitations for array of emails, validates emails, skips existing members and pending invitations, returns invitation links with unique tokens (32 bytes hex). GET /auth/accept-invite/:token validates token, checks expiry and status, returns invitation details and existing user info. POST /auth/accept-invite/:token accepts invitation, creates new user (with password/display_name) or updates existing user's orgId, marks invitation as accepted, creates session. Email sending stubbed - will be background job via BullMQ. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Department management API
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to create and manage departments.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /orgs/:id/departments returns department tree
POST /orgs/:id/departments creates department with {name, parent_id?}
PATCH /departments/:id updates name or moves to new parent
DELETE /departments/:id soft-deletes (only if no members)
POST /departments/:id/members adds user to department
DELETE /departments/:id/members/:userId removes user
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created apps/api/src/routes/departments.ts with 6 endpoints. GET /orgs/:id/departments returns hierarchical department tree for the organization. POST /orgs/:id/departments creates department with name and optional parent_id. PATCH /departments/:id updates name or moves to new parent (with circular reference detection). DELETE /departments/:id removes department only if no members or child departments. POST /departments/:id/members adds user to department with role (head/member). DELETE /departments/:id/members/:userId removes user from department. All endpoints validate UUID formats and organization membership. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: User profile API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and update my profile.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /users/me returns current user profile with departments
PATCH /users/me updates display_name, avatar_url, timezone, locale, working_hours_start, working_hours_end, phone
GET /users/:id returns public profile (name, avatar, status, department)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created apps/api/src/routes/users.ts with 3 endpoints. GET /users/me returns current user profile with department memberships (id, name, role, joinedAt). PATCH /users/me updates profile fields with validation (display_name required non-empty, working_hours validated as HH:MM or HH:MM:SS). GET /users/:id returns public profile (displayName, avatarUrl, status, departments) for users in the same organization. All endpoints require authentication via authMiddleware. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Contacts directory API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search and browse organization contacts.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /contacts?q=search returns users matching name or email (within same org)
GET /contacts/departments returns department tree with member counts
Results paginated with cursor-based pagination
Only returns users in same org_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in apps/api/src/routes/contacts.ts. GET /contacts supports ?q for search, cursor-based pagination with ?cursor and ?limit params. GET /contacts/departments returns tree with memberCount per department. Both endpoints enforce org_id scoping.

userStories.title: Chat and Message schemas

Create chat_members table: chat_id, user_id, role (enum: owner/admin/member), joined_at, muted (bool), label, last_read_message_id, primary key (chat_id, user_id)

Create messages table: id, chat_id, sender_id, type (enum: text/rich_text/code/voice/card/system), content (jsonb), thread_id (self-ref), reply_to_id (self-ref), edited_at, recalled_at, scheduled_for, created_at
Add indexes: messages(chat_id, created_at), chat_members(user_id), messages(thread_id)
Migration runs successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: content is JSONB to support different message types (text, rich_text blocks, code with language, etc.). Schema at apps/api/src/db/schema/chats.ts, migration 0005_confused_lady_deathstrike.sql

userStories.title: Messenger support schemas - reactions, pins, favorites

acceptanceCriteria: Create message_reactions table: message_id, user_id, emoji (varchar), created_at; primary key (message_id, user_id, emoji)
Create message_read_receipts table: message_id, user_id, read_at; primary key (message_id, user_id)
Create pins table: chat_id, message_id, pinned_by, pinned_at; primary key (chat_id, message_id)
Create favorites table: user_id, message_id, created_at; primary key (user_id, message_id)
Create chat_tabs table: id, chat_id, type (enum: auto/custom), name, url, position
Create announcements table: id, chat_id, content (text), author_id, created_at
Migration runs successfully
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created messenger-support.ts schema file with 6 tables: message_reactions (composite PK: message_id, user_id, emoji), message_read_receipts (composite PK: message_id, user_id), pins (composite PK: chat_id, message_id), favorites (composite PK: user_id, message_id), chat_tabs (uuid PK with chat_tab_type enum: auto/custom), announcements (uuid PK). All tables have appropriate foreign keys with cascade deletes and indexes. Migration 0006_unknown_falcon.sql applied. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: WebSocket infrastructure with Redis pub/sub
userStories.description: As a developer, I need WebSocket server with Redis pub/sub for real-time messaging.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WebSocket server to Fastify API using @fastify/websocket
On connect, authenticate via token query param, subscribe to user's chat channels in Redis
Create Redis pub/sub helper: publish(channel, event) and subscribe(channel, callback)
When a message is published to a chat channel, all connected members receive it via WebSocket
Handle disconnect: clean up subscriptions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added @fastify/websocket 11.2.0 and ioredis 5.10.0. Created src/lib/redis.ts with pub/sub helpers: publish(channel, event), subscribe(channel, callback) returning unsubscribe fn. Created src/routes/ws.ts with /ws WebSocket endpoint. Auth via ?token query param (SHA-256 hash lookup, same as REST auth). On connect: subscribes to all user's chat channels (chat:{chatId}). Messages published to chat channels are forwarded to all connected members. Tracks user connections for targeted broadcasts. Cleanup on disconnect: unsubscribes from all channels, removes from tracking maps. Graceful shutdown: closes Redis connections on SIGTERM/SIGINT. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Create DM and Group Chat APIs
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create 1:1 DMs and group chats.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /chats/dm accepts {user_id} - creates DM or returns existing DM with that user
POST /chats/group accepts {name, member_ids} - creates group chat, creator is owner
Both return full chat object with member list
DMs have type='dm', groups have type='group'
Validates all member_ids are in same org
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in apps/api/src/routes/chats.ts. POST /chats/dm creates or returns existing DM (checks for existing DM between users). POST /chats/group creates group chat with creator as owner. Both endpoints validate org membership and return full chat with member list including user details.

userStories.title: Send and receive messages API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to send messages and retrieve chat history.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /chats/:id/messages accepts {type, content} - creates message, publishes to Redis channel
GET /chats/:id/messages returns paginated messages (cursor-based, newest first)
Only chat members can send/read messages
System message auto-created when members join/leave
Updates sender's last_read_message_id on send
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in apps/api/src/routes/messages.ts. POST /chats/:id/messages creates message with type/content/thread_id/reply_to_id, validates chat membership, publishes to Redis channel (chat:{chatId}), updates sender's last_read_message_id. GET /chats/:id/messages returns cursor-based paginated messages (newest first, limit 1-100 default 50) with sender info. System messages auto-created on group creation and member addition via createSystemMessage helper. Content is JSONB: {text: 'hello'} for text, {blocks: [...]} for rich text, {code: '...', language: 'js'} for code blocks. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Chat list API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to get my list of chats sorted by recent activity.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /chats returns user's chats with: last message preview, unread count, member count, chat name/avatar
Sorted by last message timestamp (most recent first)
Includes unread count: messages after user's last_read_message_id
Supports filter query param: ?filter=dm|group|unread|muted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in apps/api/src/routes/chats.ts. GET /chats returns paginated chat list with: id, type, name (for DMs uses other user's displayName), avatarUrl (for DMs uses other user's avatar), memberCount, unreadCount (messages after last_read_message_id not sent by current user), muted flag, lastMessage (id, type, content, createdAt, senderName), lastMessageAt, createdAt, updatedAt. Sorted by lastMessageAt descending (most recent first). Supports filter query param: dm (only DM chats), group (non-DM chats), unread (chats with unreadCount > 0), muted (chats where user has muted=true). Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Chat list UI sidebar

Each chat row: avatar, name, last message preview, timestamp, unread badge
Click a chat to select it and show messages in center panel

Search box to filter chats by name

userStories.notes: Created /app/messenger page with full chat list UI. Layout modified to hide default sidebar for messenger module. Added @radix-ui/react-dialog dependency. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Chat view and message list UI

userStories.notes: Created ChatView component with full message list UI. Header shows chat avatar, name, member count, and info button. Auto-scroll to bottom on initial load and after sending messages. Infinite scroll loads older messages when scrolling near top (within 100px), with scroll position preservation. Message input with auto-resize textarea, Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline. MessageBubble component with different styling for current user vs others. System messages rendered as centered pills. userStories.title: Real-time message delivery via WebSocket

userStories.notes: Implemented useWebSocket hook (apps/web/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts) with exponential backoff reconnection (1s-30s, max 10 retries). MessengerPage subscribes to WebSocket, passes incoming messages to ChatView for real-time updates. Optimistic UI shows pending messages with lighter blue color and 'Sending...' indicator. Connection status indicator shows connected/connecting/reconnecting/disconnected states with reconnect button.

userStories.title: Typing indicators and online presence
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see when others are typing and who is online.
acceptanceCriteria: When user is typing, publish typing event to chat's Redis channel (debounced, 3s TTL)
Show 'X is typing...' indicator below message list
Online presence: heartbeat every 30s to Redis sorted set
Green dot on avatar for online users in chat member list
Presence expires after 60s without heartbeat

userStories.notes: Typing indicators are ephemeral - not persisted to database. Backend: Redis lib extended with setTyping/clearTyping/getTypingUsers (3s TTL hash storage) and updatePresence/removePresence/isUserOnline/getOnlineUsers (Redis sorted set with 60s expiry). WebSocket handles typing_start/typing_stop messages and broadcasts to chat channels. Heartbeat every 30s updates presence, cleanup runs every 30s. Frontend: useWebSocket hook extended with onTyping/onPresence callbacks and sendTypingStart/sendTypingStop methods. MessageInput triggers typing with 2.5s debounce. TypingIndicator component shows animated dots with 'X is typing...' text. OnlineIndicator component shows green dot on avatars. Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Read receipts API and UI

userStories.notes: Implemented read receipts API and UI. POST /chats/:id/read marks messages as read up to a specific message, batch inserts read receipts into message_read_receipts table, and publishes read_receipt event via WebSocket. GET /messages/:id/read-receipts returns list of who has/hasn't read the message with timestamps. UI: ReadStatusIcon component shows gray circle (unread), green check (partial), or green double-check (all read) on messages sent by current user. Click shows ReadReceiptsPopover with list of readers/non-readers. Frontend auto-marks messages as read when viewing a chat and on new incoming messages. WebSocket hook extended with onReadReceipt callback. Typecheck passes.

userStories.notes: Implemented emoji reactions with full API and UI support.
